literal: sa- (with, accompanied by) + guṇa. The lazy gloss 'with qualities' presupposes that guṇa = quality and that the relation in question is property-inherence — both presuppositions misread the metaphysics
invariant_definition:
*Brahman* described under affirmation of *guṇa*. As with *nirguṇa*, the reading depends on which sense of *guṇa* is in play — and the schools differ on which sense is in play. The *saguṇa*–*nirguṇa* opposition is not a single shared apparatus across Vedānta; it is itself a disputed doctrinal structure.

Three principal construals (see the *guṇa* and *nirguṇa* entries for background).

(1) **Sāṃkhya-derived construal: *saguṇa* as 'in association with the three strands of *prakṛti*'.** *Brahman* described as creating, sustaining, dissolving the cosmos through the *guṇa*-mediated mechanism of *prakṛti*. The Bhagavad-Gītā passages on *guṇa-pravartana* and the Lord's relation to *prakṛti*'s strands belong here.

(2) **Advaita-technical construal: *saguṇa* as *sopādhika* — *brahman* with the *upādhi* of *māyā*.** *Saguṇa brahman* is *brahman* described under its association with *māyā*-*upādhi*: the cosmic Lord who creates, sustains, dispenses *karma*-fruits. Real for the *vyāvahārika* and devotional standpoints; sublatable in *brahma-jñāna* into the *nirupādhika nirguṇa brahman*. *Saguṇa* in Advaita is thus a *cosmological-relational* description, not a property-attribution; the *guṇa*s in question are *upādhi*-borne, not intrinsic.

(3) **Viśiṣṭādvaita construal: *saguṇa* as 'eternally qualified by essential *kalyāṇa-guṇa*s'.** Brahman is *saviśeṣa* — really differentiated by infinite essential auspicious attributes that are constitutive of the Lord's *svarūpa*. Here the *guṇa*s in question are real, eternal, intrinsic — closer to the Vaiśeṣika sense of *guṇa*-as-property, but lifted out of the property-substance binary by the *aprthak-siddhi* relation: *brahman* is not separable from its *kalyāṇa-guṇa*s as a substrate is separable in thought from its accidental properties. *Saguṇa* in this construal is the highest description, not a lower one.

The single English gloss 'with qualities' silently assimilates (1), (2), and (3); the resulting flat reading misrepresents every school.

per_school.definition: *Saguṇa brahman* is *brahman* with *māyā*-*upādhi* — equivalent to *Īśvara*. The *guṇa*s here are *upādhi*-borne predicates (creator-ship, sustainer-ship, omniscience-as-cosmic-knowledge, etc.), real at the *vyāvahārika* level and sublated at the *pāramārthika* level. *Saguṇa* is thus the lower (*apara*) but real description, the proper object of *upāsana* and devotional *bhakti*. The *saguṇa*–*nirguṇa* hierarchy is foundational to Advaita and is set out in *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* 3.2.11ff.

*Brahman* is always and only *saguṇa*; there is no higher *nirguṇa* description in the Advaita sense. The *guṇa*s are *kalyāṇa-guṇa*s — infinite real auspicious attributes (*ananta-kalyāṇa-guṇa*) — eternally constitutive of Brahman-as-Viṣṇu's *svarūpa*. The relation of Brahman to its *kalyāṇa-guṇa*s is *aprthak-siddhi*, not *samavāya*: the *guṇa*s are inseparably real, not contingent properties of an underlying bare substrate. *Saguṇa* texts are read literally; *nirguṇa* texts are restricted to denying *heya-guṇa*s and *prākṛta-guṇa*s.

Viṣṇu eternally possesses infinite real *kalyāṇa-guṇa*s, each itself infinite (*ananta-ananta-kalyāṇa-guṇa*). *Saguṇa* in this sense is the only correct description of the supreme; the Advaita *saguṇa*-as-*sopādhika* construal is rejected together with the *upādhi*-doctrine. *Nirguṇa* texts deny only *prākṛta-guṇa*s.

*Saguṇa* texts describe Bhagavān (Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*) in his *svarūpa* with *svarūpa-śakti* manifest — the highest reality. The *guṇa*s are not *prākṛta* but *aprākṛta* — Kṛṣṇa's eternal *līlā*-saturated *svarūpa-guṇa*s, of which the six *bhaga*s (sovereignty, valor, glory, prosperity, knowledge, dispassion) are the canonical enumeration.

translator_note: 'With qualities', 'qualified', 'with-attributes' are the standard English glosses; all collapse the disputed metaphysics. The 'qualities' or 'attributes' in question are radically different across schools — *upādhi*-borne predicates (Advaita), *aprthak-siddhi*-related essential *kalyāṇa-guṇa*s (Viśiṣṭādvaita), eternal infinite real attributes (Dvaita), *aprākṛta svarūpa-guṇa*s (Acintya-Bhedābheda) — and 'with qualities' flattens them all into accidental property-inherence. The Sanskrit *saguṇa* must not be assimilated to the modern Western analytic notion of a 'qualified entity'. *Saguṇa* in Advaita is *not* simply 'personal' in the Christian-theological sense and *not* simply 'qualified' in the Russell-Strawson sense — it is *brahman* under cosmological-relational description by *upādhi*. *Saguṇa* in Viśiṣṭādvaita is *not* 'a substance with accidents' — it is the structurally-differentiated highest reality whose differentiations are constitutive of its identity. Best practice: leave *saguṇa* untranslated; on first use specify which school's reading is operative.
